title: gymnogramma pinnata
intro: Gymnogramma pinnata, also known as the forked fern, is a plant species that belongs to the family Asteraceae and is native to tropical Asia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of Gymnogramma pinnata include its ability to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its potential to reduce inflammation and improve wound healing. Herbal preparations of Gymnogramma pinnata include infusions and decoctions of the plant's leaves and roots, which can be used to make teas, tinctures, and topical creams for medicinal purposes.
